message.content: Read /Users/gbang/Downloads/algora/agent_prompts/lld_session_builder.md in full. This is the agent prompt that builds one complete LLD interview session. Extract the EXACT, current recipe a builder must follow: canonical source-file layout, the precise JSON tool-block contract (write_file vs run_command — exact field names, whether paths are bare/relative, content handling), the markdown section structure (§1..§9 titles), every non-negotiable quality requirement, the end-to-end generation steps in order, and any CRITICAL/gotcha notes. Be precise and complete — quote field names verbatim where they matter.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll read the file in full.

content.content:
1	# LLD Session Builder — Agent Prompt
2	
3	> **Model:** `claude-opus-4-8`
4	> **How to run:** Open Claude Code CLI in your project root, switch model with `/model claude-opus-4-8`, then type the problem statement followed by the contents of this file. OR use the Agent tool from within Claude Code with `model: "opus"` and paste this entire file as the prompt.
5	
6	You are building a **complete, ready-to-open LLD (Low Level Design) interview session** for the Algora frontend. When you are done, the user must be able to open it at `https://localhost:8002/?s=<SESSION_ID>:lld` and see the full design with working code.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## What you must produce
11	
12	Given a problem statement (e.g. "Design a Parking Lot", "Design Splitwise"), you will:
13	
14	1. **Write all Python files** into the workspace directory.
15	2. **Run `main.py`** and capture the actual output.
16	3. **Write the conversation JSON** so the frontend can open the session.
17	
18	---
19	
20	## The LLD System Prompt
21	
22	Before generating anything, read the full LLD system prompt from:
23	```
24	/Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/lld_prompt.md
25	```
26	This is the exact prompt the AI uses in production. Your output must match every section it describes (§1 through §9), including the ⏱️ 60-Minute Interview Plan at the top.
27	
28	---
29	
30	## Step 1 — Pick a session ID
31	
32	Use a fresh UUID4 for the session. The session ID format is:
33	```
34	<uuid>:lld
35	```
36	Example: `a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890:lld`
37	
38	The **slug** (used for file paths) is the session ID with every non-alphanumeric character except `-` and `_` replaced by `_`:
39	```python
40	import re
41	slug = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9_-]', '_', session_id)
42	# "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890:lld" → "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890_lld"
43	```
44	
45	---
46	
47	## Step 2 — Workspace structure
48	
49	All Python files go in:
50	```
51	/Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/workspace/<slug>/
52	```
53	
54	**CANONICAL FILE LAYOUT — use these EXACT names. Never invent `helpers.py` / `utils.py` / `service.py`.** The service file is named after the DOMAIN (e.g. `locker_service.py`, `parking_service.py`, `booking_service.py`).
55	
56	```
57	models.py             # domain entities, enums, value objects, custom exceptions (the "nouns")
58	strategies.py         # Strategy interfaces + concretes  (ONLY if §5 has a Strategy pattern)
59	gateways.py           # external-system interfaces: PaymentGateway, NotificationSink, Clock  (ONLY if §5 names one)
60	<domain>_service.py   # the orchestrator: clean single-threaded core + ThreadSafe subclass
61	main.py               # small __main__ demo driver: 3-5 cases with print(), NO test framework
62	tests/test_<domain>.py  # pytest suite: every public method, every error path, the concurrency case
63	tests/conftest.py     # adds the parent dir to sys.path so `from models import …` works in tests
64	```
65	
66	Use ~3-6 files total. Python **3.11+** idioms: `list[Foo]`, `X | None` (not `Optional[X]`), no `from __future__ import annotations`.
67	
68	### models.py must contain:
69	- All enums (`class X(IntEnum)` when ordering matters, else `class X(Enum)`)
70	- A single base exception (`<Domain>Error`) and one specific subclass per failure mode — **never mix in a bare `RuntimeError`/`Exception`/`ValueError`**
71	- All frozen dataclasses (`@dataclass(frozen=True)`) for value objects
72	- All mutable dataclasses for entities (the state-machine entity owns its own transition methods)
73	
74	### <domain>_service.py must contain:
75	- The clean single-threaded service with a `@contextmanager _lock()` no-op hook
76	- The `ThreadSafe<Domain>Service(<Domain>Service)` subclass that overrides `_lock()` with the per-key `RLock` double-lock idiom
77	- Every public method from §6, fully implemented (NO `pass`/`...` bodies)
78	- **Imports at the TOP only** — never a mid-file `import threading`
79	- **No dead code** — every method is called from `main.py` or a test
80	
81	### main.py must contain:
82	- `# Python 3.11+` as the first line
83	- A narrated demo: 3-5 cases with `print("Case N … -> OK")`, plain `assert X, message` (no `try/except False` antipattern)
84	
85	### tests/test_<domain>.py must contain (pytest-shaped):
86	- One happy-path test per public operation; one test per typed exception path; a boundary test; an idempotency test if any method is safe-to-call-twice
87	- The **concurrency test** with STRICT assertions: `len(wins) == K`, `len(set(wins)) == K` (unique reservation ids), `len({w.compartment_id …}) == K` (unique resources — catches the silent collision bug), every loss is the typed error, and the count of in-RESERVED/OCCUPIED resources == K. Use `threading.Barrier` so all N threads hit the critical section at one instant. `pytest.raises(<TypedError>)` — never bare `Exception`.
88	
89	---
90	
91	## Step 3 — Generate the full §1–§9 LLD response
92	
93	Using the system prompt from `lld_prompt.md`, generate the complete LLD response as markdown. This is what the user will READ in the frontend — it must be complete, not abbreviated. Match the CURRENT structure exactly:
94	
95	- **⏱️ 60-Minute Interview Plan** — table (phase | what you do | mode) + the Mode A (whiteboard, no run) vs Mode B (coding env, run) note.
96	- **§1 Understanding the Problem & Clarifying Questions** — 1.1 plain-words restatement (coffee-chat tone), 1.2 **candidate-LED dialogue** (`**You:**` proposes an assumption/scope-cut + asks to confirm, `**Interviewer:**` ~70% agrees, italic reaction line), 1.3 Final Requirements (numbered functional + "Out of scope" list) + 💬 opener.
97	- **§2 Actors & Core Flows** — bulleted actors + 3-5 one-line happy-path flows. No diagram here.
98	- **§3 Core Entities (accept/reject)** — 3.1 noun walkthrough accepting/**rejecting** each candidate noun with a reason, 3.2 entity table (Entity | Responsibility | Key fields typed | Invariant) + enums block, 3.3 🎙️ spoken-intro paragraph that names the rejected nouns.
99	- **§4 Class Design** — 4.1 per-class derivation (role → state-derivation table → operations-derivation table → design-choices Q&A), 4.2 consolidated `classDiagram` (draw-vs-say split) + relationships + Pattern Map + 🎙️ box-by-box + **⚠️ PROACTIVE CONCURRENCY FLAG**.
100	- **§5 Design Patterns & Principles** — 5.1 Information Expert first, 5.2 applied patterns (1-3, each earned, with 💬 layman gloss), 5.3 SOLID tied to named classes, 5.4 EARN YOUR PATTERNS rule.
101	- **§5.5 Core Algorithms** — A key insight (insight + why-it-works + what-breaks-without-it), B per-operation pseudocode + STORY-driven 🎙️ scripts + a **DRY RUN** of the pipeline method with a state-change table, C data-structure choices (4 cols incl. rejected alternative), D tricky logic (🧠 Claim / 🔬 Proof / 🛠️ Fix), E ONE "❌ what I'd write WRONG first" anti-example, F narration-order table.
102	- **§6 Implementation** — **§6.0 Live Writing Order** (9 phases + 7 golden rules + TL;DR time table + mid-flight situations), then orchestrator SKELETON first, then each method in the 6-block format (role / core-logic bullets / edge-case bullets / code / reasoning PROSE / 🎙️+⚠️), ONE **Approach Comparison** for the trickiest helper, helpers written (not skipped), and a **Verification** subsection with full-lifecycle state-table dry runs.
103	- **§7 Putting It Together** — 7.1 `main.py` narrated demo output, 7.2 `tests/` pytest suite + the actual `pytest -q` output. Both green.
104	- **§8 Key Flow** — a `sequenceDiagram` of the most interesting flow + 🎙️ arrow-by-arrow narration + why-it-shows-the-design-is-good.
105	- **§9 Concurrency, Thread-Safety, Edge Cases & Extensibility** — 4-step verbal race walkthrough, **CONCURRENCY VOCABULARY table**, **CONCURRENCY FOLLOW-UP Q&A** (why RLock, why not global, deadlock, pessimistic vs optimistic, …), the clean→thread-safe diff (Part A hook+subclass, Part B exact modified method, Part C barrier test proof), edge-cases-handled list, **Extensibility** (level-calibration note + summary table + 2-3 TWIST walkthroughs with minimal code diffs), and the hardest follow-up Q&A (concurrency/distributed/scale — distinct from the twists).
106	
107	**CRITICAL RULES FOR CODE IN §6:**
108	- NO `...` ANYWHERE. No abbreviations. No `# ... rest of implementation`.
109	- Every method body must be complete — every line, every real argument, every real error message, every real return value.
110	- Code blocks tagged ` ```python copy ` so the frontend renders a copy button.
111	- The §6 narration shows code method-by-method (teaching); the FULL files also ship as Write tool blocks (the frontend's LLD "Complete Code Implementation" tabbed viewer renders them from those `write_file` captures).
112	
113	---
114	
115	## Step 4 — Run main.py AND pytest, capture output
116	
117	```bash
118	cd /Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/workspace/<slug>
119	python3 main.py
120	python3 -m pytest -q
121	```
122	
123	Capture the exact stdout from BOTH. The `main.py` output goes into the `tool_run` Bash block; the `pytest -q` output goes into the `tool_pytest` Bash block. Both must be green — if either errors, fix the CODE (not the test) and re-run before continuing.
124	
125	---
126	
127	## Step 5 — Write the conversation JSON
128	
129	File path:
130	```
131	/Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/data/conversations/<slug>.json
132	```
133	
134	### Tool-block format (CRITICAL — the frontend keys off these exact names):
135	- File blocks: `"name": "write_file"`, `"input": {"path": "<bare path>", "content": "..."}`. The `path` is RELATIVE to the workspace (`models.py`, `tests/test_booking.py`) — **NOT** `workspace/<slug>/models.py`, and **NOT** `"Write"`/`"file_path"`. The frontend's LLD "Complete Code Implementation" tabbed viewer is built from these `write_file` blocks, so every file the candidate should see must be a `write_file` block.
136	- Run blocks: `"name": "run_command"`, `"input": {"command": "python3 main.py"}` — bare command, no `cd`.
137	- Also populate `messages` (Anthropic format) so follow-ups have context: a user message with the problem text and an assistant message whose single text block is the full §1–§9 markdown.
138	
139	### JSON structure:
140	```json
141	{
142	  "session_id": "<uuid>:lld",
143	  "mode": "lld",
144	  "title": "Design a <Problem Name>",
145	  "created_at": <unix timestamp>,
146	  "updated_at": <unix timestamp + 180>,
147	  "messages": [
148	    {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "<problem statement>"}]},
149	    {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "<THE FULL §1-§9 MARKDOWN>"}]}
150	  ],
151	  "transcript": [
152	    {
153	      "role": "user",
154	      "text": "Design a <Problem Name>",
155	      "images": 0
156	    },
157	    {
158	      "role": "assistant",
159	      "thinking": "",
160	      "blocks": [
161	        {
162	          "k": "text",
163	          "md": "<THE FULL §1-§9 MARKDOWN RESPONSE>"
164	        },
165	        {
166	          "k": "tool",
167	          "id": "tool_write_0",
168	          "name": "write_file",
169	          "input": {
170	            "path": "models.py",
171	            "content": "<full file content>"
172	          },
173	          "output": "File created successfully.",
174	          "is_error": false
175	        },
176	        {
177	          "k": "tool",
178	          "id": "tool_write_1",
179	          "name": "write_file",
180	          "input": {
181	            "path": "<domain>_service.py",
182	            "content": "<full file content>"
183	          },
184	          "output": "File created successfully.",
185	          "is_error": false
186	        },
187	        {
188	          "k": "tool",
189	          "id": "tool_write_2",
190	          "name": "write_file",
191	          "input": {
192	            "path": "main.py",
193	            "content": "<full file content>"
194	          },
195	          "output": "File created successfully.",
196	          "is_error": false
197	        },
198	        {
199	          "k": "tool",
200	          "id": "tool_write_3",
201	          "name": "write_file",
202	          "input": {
203	            "path": "tests/test_<domain>.py",
204	            "content": "<full pytest file content>"
205	          },
206	          "output": "File created successfully.",
207	          "is_error": false
208	        },
209	        {
210	          "k": "tool",
211	          "id": "tool_write_4",
212	          "name": "write_file",
213	          "input": {
214	            "path": "tests/conftest.py",
215	            "content": "<conftest that adds parent dir to sys.path>"
216	          },
217	          "output": "File created successfully.",
218	          "is_error": false
219	        },
220	        {
221	          "k": "tool",
222	          "id": "tool_run_main",
223	          "name": "run_command",
224	          "input": {
225	            "command": "python3 main.py"
226	          },
227	          "output": "<actual stdout from running main.py>",
228	          "is_error": false
229	        },
230	        {
231	          "k": "tool",
232	          "id": "tool_run_pytest",
233	          "name": "run_command",
234	          "input": {
235	            "command": "python3 -m pytest -q"
236	          },
237	          "output": "<actual pytest stdout — all green>",
238	          "is_error": false
239	        }
240	      ],
241	      "usage": {
242	        "input_tokens": 9000,
243	        "output_tokens": 16000,
244	        "cache_read_input_tokens": 40000,
245	        "cache_creation_input_tokens": 0
246	      }
247	    }
248	  ]
249	}
250	```
251	
252	### Important slug rule:
253	The JSON filename and the session_id field inside it must be consistent:
254	```python
255	import re, json, pathlib
256	
257	session_id = "<uuid>:lld"
258	slug = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9_-]', '_', session_id)
259	# slug == "<uuid>_lld"   ← this is the filename
260	# session_id == "<uuid>:lld"  ← this goes INSIDE the JSON as the "session_id" field
261	```
262	
263	Write the JSON using Python to avoid encoding issues:
264	```python
265	import json, pathlib
266	out = pathlib.Path('data/conversations') / f'{slug}.json'
267	out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
268	out.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding='utf-8')
269	```
270	
271	---
272	
273	## Step 6 — Verify
274	
275	After writing the JSON, verify it loads correctly:
276	```python
277	import json, sys
278	sys.path.insert(0, '.')
279	from backend.config import session_slug, CONV_DIR
280	
281	sid = '<uuid>:lld'
282	slug = session_slug(sid)
283	p = CONV_DIR / f'{slug}.json'
284	assert p.exists(), f"File not found: {p}"
285	d = json.loads(p.read_text())
286	assert d['session_id'] == sid
287	assert d['mode'] == 'lld'
288	print('OK — open at: https://localhost:8002/?s=' + sid)
289	```
290	
291	---
292	
293	## Quality checklist before finishing
294	
295	- [ ] Canonical file layout: `models.py`, `<domain>_service.py`, `main.py`, `tests/test_<domain>.py`, `tests/conftest.py` (+ `strategies.py`/`gateways.py` only if §5 needs them) — NO `service.py`/`utils.py`/`helpers.py`
296	- [ ] Python 3.11+ idioms (`list[Foo]`, `X | None`); no `from __future__ import annotations`; imports at top only (no mid-file `import threading`)
297	- [ ] `python3 main.py` exits 0 with all cases printing "OK"
298	- [ ] `python3 -m pytest -q` is fully green
299	- [ ] Concurrency test asserts UNIQUE resources (`len({…compartment_id…}) == K`), not just the win count
300	- [ ] Single domain exception base; no bare `RuntimeError`/`Exception` raised from own code; no dead code
301	- [ ] No `...` in any code in §6 — every method is complete
302	- [ ] §6 includes §6.0 Live Writing Order, orchestrator skeleton-first, the 6-block per-method format, ONE Approach Comparison, and a Verification dry-run subsection
303	- [ ] §9 includes the Concurrency Vocabulary table, the Concurrency Follow-up Q&A, and 2-3 Extensibility TWIST walkthroughs
304	- [ ] Conversation JSON exists at `data/conversations/<slug>.json`
305	- [ ] `session_id` inside JSON matches the `:lld` format (with colon, not underscore)
306	- [ ] Slug in filename uses `_lld` (colon → underscore)
307	- [ ] All tool blocks use `write_file` (×5-6, bare relative `path`) + `run_command` (×2) — never `Write`/`Bash`/`file_path`/`workspace/<slug>/…`
308	- [ ] The full §1–§9 markdown is in the first text block
309	- [ ] `python3 -c "from backend.config import session_slug, CONV_DIR; ..."` confirms file is loadable
310

content.text: I have read the file in full. Now I'll extract the exact recipe and return it via StructuredOutput.

fileLayout: models.py — domain entities, enums, value objects, custom exceptions (the "nouns")
strategies.py — Strategy interfaces + concretes (ONLY if §5 has a Strategy pattern)
gateways.py — external-system interfaces: PaymentGateway, NotificationSink, Clock (ONLY if §5 names one)

<domain>_service.py — the orchestrator: clean single-threaded core + ThreadSafe subclass (named after the DOMAIN, e.g. locker_service.py / parking_service.py / booking_service.py — NEVER service.py/utils.py/helpers.py)
main.py — small __main__ demo driver: 3-5 cases with print(), NO test framework
tests/test_<domain>.py — pytest suite: every public method, every error path, the concurrency case
tests/conftest.py — adds the parent dir to sys.path so `from models import …` works in tests
Total ~3-6 files. Workspace root: /Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/workspace/<slug>/ where slug = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9_-]', '_', session_id)
input.jsonToolBlockContract:
EXACT field names — the frontend keys off these verbatim.

FILE BLOCKS (×5-6): "name": "write_file", "input": {"path": "<bare path>", "content": "..."}. The `path` is RELATIVE to the workspace — e.g. "models.py", "tests/test_booking.py" — NOT "workspace/<slug>/models.py", and the name is NOT "Write" and the key is NOT "file_path". Every file the candidate should see MUST be a write_file block (the LLD "Complete Code Implementation" tabbed viewer is built from them).

RUN BLOCKS (×2): "name": "run_command", "input": {"command": "python3 main.py"} — bare command, no `cd`. The two commands are "python3 main.py" and "python3 -m pytest -q".

Each tool block inside transcript[].blocks has shape: {"k": "tool", "id": "<id>", "name": "write_file"|"run_command", "input": {...}, "output": "<actual stdout / 'File created successfully.'>", "is_error": false}. Example ids: tool_write_0, tool_write_1, tool_write_2, tool_write_3, tool_write_4 (write_file blocks), tool_run_main and tool_run_pytest (run_command blocks). main.py stdout goes in the tool_run_main / tool_run block; pytest -q stdout goes in the tool_run_pytest block; both must be green.

TOP-LEVEL JSON: {"session_id": "<uuid>:lld", "mode": "lld", "title": "Design a <Problem Name>", "created_at": <unix ts>, "updated_at": <unix ts + 180>, "messages": [...], "transcript": [...]}. messages is Anthropic format: a user message {"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"<problem>"}]} and an assistant message {"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"<FULL §1-§9 markdown>"}]}. transcript[0] is {"role":"user","text":"Design a <Problem Name>","images":0}; transcript[1] is {"role":"assistant","thinking":"","blocks":[ {first block k:"text", md:"<FULL §1-§9 markdown>"}, then the write_file + run_command tool blocks ], "usage": {"input_tokens":9000,"output_tokens":16000,"cache_read_input_tokens":40000,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0}}. Text blocks use keys "k":"text" and "md".

FILENAME vs FIELD: JSON file is /Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/data/conversations/<slug>.json where slug uses _lld (colon→underscore); the session_id field INSIDE the JSON keeps the colon form <uuid>:lld. Write via Python: out.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding='utf-8').
sectionStructure: ⏱️ 60-Minute Interview Plan (at the very top) — table (phase | what you do | mode) + the Mode A (whiteboard, no run) vs Mode B (coding env, run) note

§1 Understanding the Problem & Clarifying Questions — 1.1 plain-words restatement (coffee-chat tone); 1.2 candidate-LED dialogue (**You:** proposes assumption/scope-cut + asks to confirm, **Interviewer:** ~70% agrees, italic reaction line); 1.3 Final Requirements (numbered functional + "Out of scope" list) + 💬 opener
§2 Actors & Core Flows — bulleted actors + 3-5 one-line happy-path flows. No diagram here

§3 Core Entities (accept/reject) — 3.1 noun walkthrough accepting/rejecting each candidate noun with a reason; 3.2 entity table (Entity | Responsibility | Key fields typed | Invariant) + enums block; 3.3 🎙️ spoken-intro paragraph that names the rejected nouns

§4 Class Design — 4.1 per-class derivation (role → state-derivation table → operations-derivation table → design-choices Q&A); 4.2 consolidated classDiagram (draw-vs-say split) + relationships + Pattern Map + 🎙️ box-by-box + ⚠️ PROACTIVE CONCURRENCY FLAG
§5 Design Patterns & Principles — 5.1 Information Expert first; 5.2 applied patterns (1-3, each earned, with 💬 layman gloss); 5.3 SOLID tied to named classes; 5.4 EARN YOUR PATTERNS rule

§5.5 Core Algorithms — A key insight (insight + why-it-works + what-breaks-without-it); B per-operation pseudocode + STORY-driven 🎙️ scripts + a DRY RUN of the pipeline method with a state-change table; C data-structure choices (4 cols incl. rejected alternative); D tricky logic (🧠 Claim / 🔬 Proof / 🛠️ Fix); E ONE "❌ what I'd write WRONG first" anti-example; F narration-order table

§6 Implementation — §6.0 Live Writing Order (9 phases + 7 golden rules + TL;DR time table + mid-flight situations), then orchestrator SKELETON first, then each method in the 6-block format (role / core-logic bullets / edge-case bullets / code / reasoning PROSE / 🎙️+⚠️), ONE Approach Comparison for the trickiest helper, helpers written (not skipped), and a Verification subsection with full-lifecycle state-table dry runs
§7 Putting It Together — 7.1 main.py narrated demo output; 7.2 tests/ pytest suite + the actual `pytest -q` output. Both green
§8 Key Flow — a sequenceDiagram of the most interesting flow + 🎙️ arrow-by-arrow narration + why-it-shows-the-design-is-good

§9 Concurrency, Thread-Safety, Edge Cases & Extensibility — 4-step verbal race walkthrough, CONCURRENCY VOCABULARY table, CONCURRENCY FOLLOW-UP Q&A (why RLock, why not global, deadlock, pessimistic vs optimistic), the clean→thread-safe diff (Part A hook+subclass, Part B exact modified method, Part C barrier test proof), edge-cases-handled list, Extensibility (level-calibration note + summary table + 2-3 TWIST walkthroughs with minimal code diffs), and the hardest follow-up Q&A (concurrency/distributed/scale — distinct from the twists)
qualityRequirements: Canonical file layout enforced: models.py, <domain>_service.py, main.py, tests/test_<domain>.py, tests/conftest.py (+ strategies.py/gateways.py only if §5 needs them) — NO service.py/utils.py/helpers.py; service file named after the DOMAIN
Python 3.11+ idioms: list[Foo], X | None (not Optional[X]); NO `from __future__ import annotations`; imports at TOP only (no mid-file `import threading`)

models.py: all enums (IntEnum when ordering matters, else Enum); a SINGLE base exception <Domain>Error with one specific subclass per failure mode — never mix in a bare RuntimeError/Exception/ValueError; frozen dataclasses (@dataclass(frozen=True)) for value objects; mutable dataclasses for entities (state-machine entity owns its own transition methods)

<domain>_service.py: clean single-threaded service with a @contextmanager _lock() no-op hook; a ThreadSafe<Domain>Service(<Domain>Service) subclass overriding _lock() with the per-key RLock double-lock idiom; every public method from §6 fully implemented (NO pass/... bodies); no dead code (every method called from main.py or a test)
main.py: `# Python 3.11+` as the first line; narrated demo of 3-5 cases with print("Case N … -> OK") and plain `assert X, message` (no try/except False antipattern)

Concurrency test with STRICT assertions: len(wins) == K, len(set(wins)) == K (unique reservation ids), len({w.compartment_id …}) == K (UNIQUE resources — catches the silent collision bug), every loss is the typed error, and count of in-RESERVED/OCCUPIED resources == K; use threading.Barrier so all N threads hit the critical section at one instant; pytest.raises(<TypedError>) — never bare Exception
python3 main.py exits 0 with all cases printing OK; python3 -m pytest -q fully green; if either errors, fix the CODE (not the test) and re-run before continuing
NO `...` ANYWHERE in §6 code; no abbreviations, no `# ... rest of implementation`; every method body complete (every line, real argument, real error message, real return value)
Code blocks tagged ```python copy so the frontend renders a copy button

The §6 narration shows code method-by-method (teaching); the FULL files ALSO ship as write_file tool blocks — the frontend's LLD 'Complete Code Implementation' tabbed viewer renders from those write_file captures

Output must match every section (§1 through §9) of the production LLD system prompt at /Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/lld_prompt.md, including the ⏱️ 60-Minute Interview Plan at the top; the full §1–§9 markdown must be in the first text block (complete, not abbreviated)
§6 includes §6.0 Live Writing Order, orchestrator skeleton-first, the 6-block per-method format, ONE Approach Comparison, and a Verification dry-run subsection
§9 includes the Concurrency Vocabulary table, the Concurrency Follow-up Q&A, and 2-3 Extensibility TWIST walkthroughs
recipeSteps: Step 0 (prereq) — Read the full LLD system prompt at /Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/lld_prompt.md before generating anything; the output must match its §1–§9 structure
Step 1 — Pick a session ID: a fresh UUID4 in the format <uuid>:lld. Compute slug = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9_-]', '_', session_id) (so :lld → _lld)

Step 2 — Set up workspace structure under /Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/workspace/<slug>/ using the EXACT canonical file names (~3-6 files), with the models.py / <domain>_service.py / main.py / tests content rules
Step 3 — Generate the full §1–§9 LLD response as markdown (the complete, non-abbreviated text the user reads), matching the current structure: ⏱️ 60-Minute Interview Plan + §1..§9 exactly

Step 4 — Run BOTH `cd /Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/workspace/<slug>` then `python3 main.py` and `python3 -m pytest -q`; capture exact stdout from both (main → tool_run Bash block, pytest → tool_pytest Bash block); both must be green or fix the CODE and re-run

Step 5 — Write the conversation JSON to /Users/anshullkgarg/Desktop/projects/claude-gpt/data/conversations/<slug>.json with the documented structure: session_id (colon form), mode 'lld', title, created_at/updated_at, messages (Anthropic format), and transcript (text block first, then write_file ×5-6 + run_command ×2). Write via Python json.dumps(..., ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)

Step 6 — Verify the JSON loads: `from backend.config import session_slug, CONV_DIR`, assert the file exists at CONV_DIR/<slug>.json, assert d['session_id'] == sid and d['mode'] == 'lld', then print the open URL https://localhost:8002/?s=<uuid>:lld

Final — Run the Quality checklist before finishing (file layout, 3.11 idioms, green main/pytest, unique-resource concurrency assert, single exception base, no ... in §6, §6.0/§9 required subsections, JSON exists, session_id colon form, slug _lld filename, all blocks use write_file/run_command with bare relative paths, full markdown in first text block, config loadability check)
gotchas: CRITICAL tool-block naming: frontend keys off EXACT names — use write_file / run_command, never Write / Bash. Use `path` (bare/relative) not `file_path`, and `command` not other keys. Bare relative paths like models.py and tests/test_booking.py — NEVER workspace/<slug>/models.py.

Slug vs session_id mismatch trap: the JSON FILENAME uses _lld (colon replaced by underscore) but the session_id field INSIDE the JSON keeps the colon (<uuid>:lld). The open URL also uses the colon form.
CANONICAL FILE LAYOUT is mandatory — never invent helpers.py / utils.py / service.py; the service file is named after the DOMAIN (locker_service.py, parking_service.py, booking_service.py).
NO `...` anywhere in §6 code, no abbreviations like `# ... rest of implementation`; every method body fully implemented (no pass/... bodies even in the service).
Single domain exception base only — never raise a bare RuntimeError/Exception/ValueError from your own code; one specific subclass per failure mode.
Imports at TOP only — never a mid-file `import threading`. Python 3.11+ idioms (list[Foo], X | None); do NOT add `from __future__ import annotations`.

Concurrency test must assert UNIQUE RESOURCES (len({…compartment_id…}) == K), not just the win count — this catches the silent collision bug; use threading.Barrier and pytest.raises(<TypedError>), never bare Exception.
If main.py or pytest errors, fix the CODE — not the test — and re-run before continuing. Both must be green.
strategies.py is included ONLY if §5 has a Strategy pattern; gateways.py ONLY if §5 names an external-system interface (PaymentGateway/NotificationSink/Clock).
No dead code: every public method must be exercised by main.py or a test.
Run model is claude-opus-4-8; the LLD response is the long-form artifact the user READS in the frontend at https://localhost:8002/?s=<uuid>:lld — it must be complete, not summarized.
Code blocks for §6 must be tagged ```python copy (with the `copy` token) so the frontend renders a copy button.

The full §1–§9 markdown must appear BOTH in messages[1] (assistant text, Anthropic format) AND as the first text block (k:text, md:...) of transcript[1].blocks, so follow-ups have context and the reader sees it.
